mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Feb 2015 indicates near-zero rates
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising household income expectations, which boost consumer confidence and discretionary spending, translating to positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,223k as of Feb 2015 shows continued post-recession employment growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal increased residential investment and forward-looking builder confidence, which amplifies multiplier effects through construction employment and materials demand, generating positive equity returns within 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 581k units as of Jan 2015 reflects post-crisis housing recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST rises > 5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and signals recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice downside tail risk.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 as of Jan 2015 shows modest industrial activity
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while unemployment remains elevated above 5.5%, the combination signals weakening consumer demand and labor market slack, triggering a downward repricing of equity valuations over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of Feb 2015 shows labor market slack persists
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with incomplete labor market healing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, the real interest rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower discount rates and reduced real debt burdens, driving positive 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.47 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Jan-Feb 2015
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with the negative real rate condition (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% combined with declining month-over-month payroll growth signals labor market deterioration and rising recession probability, which compresses equity risk appetite and produces negative 20-day returns as investors reduce exposure to cyclical equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of Feb 2015 indicates persistent slack
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized signals subtrend economic expansion and rising slack in resource utilization, which compresses earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-term growth downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,892.5B as of Q4 2014 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with subtrend GDP growth (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
